parsed.production_notes: The video is composed of live-action, user-generated style travel footage. The camera movements, natural lighting, and realistic human features indicate it is human-recorded.

characters.appearance: Young woman with a slim build and light complexion. She has long, wavy brown hair.
characters.wardrobe: White long-sleeve top and loose olive green cargo pants.

scenes.setting: Paved city walkway at night, modern buildings, streetlights, warm artificial lighting.

panels.panel_visual_description: Character A is walking backwards down a paved city walkway at night. The background features modern buildings with glass facades, streetlights, and small trees. The lighting is artificial, coming from the buildings and street lamps, creating a warm glow. The camera follows her as she walks, keeping her centered in the frame. She is smiling and looking towards the camera.

scenes.visual_description: The scene shows Character A walking backwards on a city street at night, smiling at the camera. A large block of text is overlaid on the center of the screen, describing her experience learning Spanish.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Unknown speaker says: Ella hace todo por seducirme y yo voy, voy, voy. [She does everything to seduce me and I go, go, go.]
Unknown speaker says: Haciendo lo que ella me pide y yo voy, voy, voy. [Doing what she asks of me and I go, go, go.]
Unknown speaker says: Porque fue la que siempre quise y yo voy, voy, voy. [Because she was the one I always wanted and I go, go, go.]
Unknown speaker says: Si estoy... [If I am...]
scenes.on_screen_text:
I switched from Duolingo to Speak.
I feel like I hacked my brain?
In 72 hours, I was ordering tacos in Spanish.
In 10 days, I understood the full Bad Bunny album.
Using the immersion method actually helps you speak words and hear sounds without thinking.
I started the app 2.5 months ago and today I'm talking like a native.

scenes.setting: Various outdoor travel locations including a street, a waterfall, a grassy hillside, and inside a jeep. Daylight.

panels.panel_visual_description: A fast-paced montage of travel clips. It starts with a man walking past a white building with a barred window. Then, a first-person view of riding a scooter down a palm-tree-lined road at sunset. Next, a wide shot of a large, powerful waterfall. This is followed by a high-angle shot of a person walking behind a large flock of sheep on a grassy hillside. Finally, a view from inside a moving jeep, showing the driver and a passenger pointing out the window.

scenes.visual_description: A montage of travel clips showing various scenic locations and activities. Overlaid on the video is a comparison chart with two columns. The left column has a blurred green owl logo and red 'X' marks next to negative language learning experiences. The right column has a blue logo and green checkmarks next to positive experiences.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Unknown speaker says: Te miran las fotos de cuando te tuve. [They look at the photos of when I had you.]
Unknown speaker says: Te di los besos y abrazos las veces que pude. [I gave you the kisses and hugs the times I could.]
Unknown speaker says: ¿Qué? [What?]
scenes.on_screen_text:
[Blurred green owl logo]
X Random word memorization
X Embarrassing yourself with locals
X Recognizing words but unable to speak them
[Blue soundwave logo]
✓ Responding smoothly without overthinking
✓ Ordering perfectly at restaurants
✓ Understanding music and shows without help

scenes.setting: Subway station, inside a jeep, and a paved road. Daylight.

panels.panel_visual_description: Another montage of travel clips. It begins with a first-person view walking through a modern, brightly lit subway station. Then, a shot from inside a moving jeep, looking out at the passing scenery. Finally, Character A is seen walking away from the camera down a paved road with cars parked on the side.

scenes.visual_description: A continuation of the travel montage, featuring a subway station, a jeep ride, and a woman walking down a road. A large block of text is overlaid on the screen, criticizing traditional language learning apps and promoting the immersion method.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Unknown speaker says: Y ese cuerpito que tú tienes, el traje de baño chiquitito te queda. [And that little body you have, the tiny swimsuit fits you.]
Unknown speaker says: Es blanquita, con el sol y de una ya se pone morena. [She's white, with the sun and she immediately gets tan.]
scenes.on_screen_text: Nobody tells you that the way that most apps teach Spanish doesn't work. That streaks and memorization of random words won't help you in real life. That children and babies don't learn how to speak by conjugating verbs. And that if your mouth doesn't learn to actually make the words and hear the sounds without thinking, you'll never be able to actually communicate. That's why language experts created this app using the immersion method, that is proven to help you learn 3.7x faster. I started the app 2.5 months ago and today I'm speaking like a native. I just wish I would have found it sooner.
